Response: Robotics and Perception Group (RPG), University of Zurich and ETH Zurich; lead developers: Yunlong Song (ETH Zurich RPG), Selim Nusbaum, and team; PI: Davide Scaramuzza (ETH Zurich RPG director)

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Flexible modular quadrotor simulator for learning and control research; provides rendering-based environment (Unity3D rendering engine) and physics simulation for training reinforcement learning agents on drone racing and agile flight tasks; modular architecture separates rendering (Unity) from RL environment (Python/C++) for flexibility in fast or photorealistic simulation modes

column4: Flightmare was designed specifically for the drone RL research community. Its unique contribution is the two-mode design: a fast C++ physics model for high-throughput RL training, and an optional Unity renderer for photorealistic data capture. The 1.3k stars reflect impact in the drone RL community. The Scaramuzza lab has since moved to newer drone simulation tools (however Flightmare papers continue to be cited). Dependency on Unity3D for the renderer is a complexity factor.
